title: Billiards device and method
abstract: A measurement device (16) and method for analyzing a billiards shot using an accelerometer (25) for generating acceleration data, an indication system (24), a microprocessor (26) for calculating statistical data, a power source (22), and a housing (20). The device (16), when powered, detects when the player has struck a billiards ball with a billiards cue. The microprocessor (26) then acquires acceleration data from the accelerometer (25) and outputs a signal if the data exceeds a predetermined threshold, thus alerting the player. The device (16) brings the player's attention to improper billiards cue movement, allowing the player to improve his or her stroke consistency.
claims: Claims What is claimed is: 1. A measurement device (16) for analyzing a billiards shot, comprising : at least one accelerometer (25) operably secured to a billiards cue (2); a microprocessor (26) in communication with the accelerometer; and an indicator. 2. The measurement device (16) of claim 1, further comprising a power source (22) . 3. The measurement device (16) of claim 1, further comprising a housing (20) . 4. The measurement device (16) of claim 3, wherein the accelerometer (25) and microprocessor (26) are disposed within the housing (20) . 5. The measurement device (16) of claim 4, further comprising a power source (22) . 6. The measurement device (16) of claim 1 wherein the indicator comprises a display (24) . 7. The measurement device (16) of claim 1 wherein the indicator comprises an audible tone generator or speaker. 8. The measurement device (16) of claim 1 wherein the indicator comprises a vibration motor (27) . 9. The measurement device (16) of claim 1 wherein the indicator is enclosed within the housing (20) . 10. The measurement device (16) of claim 9 further comprising a power source (22) . 11. A method for analyzing a billiards shot, comprising: measuring the motion of a billiards cue (2) with an accelerometer (25) operably secured thereto; analyzing the motion of the billiards cue (2) with a microprocessor (26) in communication with the accelerometer; comparing the motion of the billiards cue (2) to a pattern; and generating a notification depending on whether the motion of the billiards cue (2) conforms to the pattern. 12. The method of claim 11, further comprising the step of providing a power source (22) . 13. The method of claim 11, further comprising the step of providing a housing (20) . 14. The method of claim 13, wherein the accelerometer (25) and microprocessor (26) are disposed within the housing (20) . 15. The method of claim 14, further comprising the step of providing a power source (22) . 16. The method of claim 11 wherein the notification is provided via a display (24) . 17. The method of claim 11 wherein the notification is provided via an audible tone generator or speaker. 18. The method of claim 11 wherein the notification is provided via a vibration motor (27) . 19. The method of claim 11 wherein the notification is provided via an indicator enclosed within a housing (20) . 20. The method of claim 19 further comprising the step of providing a power source (22) .
